properties.remark: *** 1 FATAL, 30 INJ *** A TORNADO AFFECTED FULTONDALE, PRODUCING EF-3 DAMAGE ACROSS A FEW NEIGHBORHOODS. HOMES WERE HEAVILY DAMAGED OR DESTROYED, WITH THOUSANDS OF TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. THERE WAS 1 FATALITY AND 30 PLUS INJURIES.

*** 1 FATAL, 28 INJ *** UPDATE...AT LEAST ONE FATALITY HAS BEEN CONFIRMED ALONG WITH MAJOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE IN FULTONDALE. AT LEAST 28 INJURIES HAVE BEEN REPORTED THUS FAR. TDS WAS OBSERVED ON KBMX RADAR AT THE TIME OF THE TORNADO.

*** 1 FATAL, 30 INJ *** INJURY UPDATE...AT LEAST ONE FATALITY HAS BEEN CONFIRMED ALONG WITH MAJOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE IN FULTONDALE. DAMAGE HAS ALSO BEEN REPORTED FARTHER NORTHEAST INTO CENTER POINT. AT LEAST 30 INJURIES HAVE BEEN REPORTED THUS FAR. TDS WAS OBSERVED ON KBMX RADAR AT THE TIME OF THE TORNADO.

*** 1 FATAL *** UPDATE...AT LEAST ONE FATALITY HAS BEEN CONFIRMED ALONG WITH MAJOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE IN FULTONDALE. MULTIPLE INJURIES ARE ALSO BEING REPORTED, BUT A SPECIFIC NUMBER IS UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME. TDS WAS OBSERVED ON KBMX RADAR AT THE TIME OF THE TORNADO.
SEVERAL REPORTS OF DOWNED POWER LINES AND STRUCTURAL DAMAGE THROUGHOUT FULTONDALE. TDS ON KBMX RADAR.
SEVERAL PORTABLE STORAGE UNITS BLOWN ACROSS THE STREET AND DEMOLISHED. WIND GUSTS ESTIMATED AT 40-50 MPH.
METAL ROOF BLOWN OFF BUILDING ON LANE DRIVE. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 40 TO 50 MPH.
A COUPLE REPORTS OF HAIL AT QUARTER SIZE IN THE GADSDEN AREA.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS HAIL REPORT FROM 1 ENE LAMAR COUNTY AIRPORT. MOSTLY DIME SIZE HAIL, BUT A FEW TO QUARTER SIZE NEAR THE LAMAR COUNTY AIRPORT. HAIL LASTED ONLY A MINUTE OR TWO.
MOSTLY DIME SIZE HAIL, BUT A FEW TO QUARTER SIZE NEAR THE LAMAR COUNTY AIRPORT. HAIL LASTED ONLY A MINUTE OR TWO.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS HAIL REPORT FROM 1 ENE LAMAR COUNTY AIRPORT. MOSTLY DIME SIZE HAIL, BUT A FEW TO QUARTER SIZE NEAR THE LAMAR COUNTY AIRPORT. HAIL LASTED ONLY A MINUTE OR TWO.
MOSTLY DIME SIZE HAIL, BUT A FEW TO QUARTER SIZE NEAR THE LAMAR COUNTY AIRPORT. HAIL LASTED ONLY A MINUTE OR TWO.

A NWS SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED DAMAGE FROM A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO IN SOUTHERN SUMTER COUNTY. THE TORNADO BEGAN AND ENDED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF WALKER ROAD AND HIGHWAY 17. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF UPROOTED TREES, BROKEN LARGE TREE BRANCHES, AND SOME SHINGLE DAMAGE TO A RESIDENCE.

*** 2 INJ *** EF2 TORNADO DAMAGE PATH WAS 5.08 MILES LONG AND 1000 YARDS WIDE AT ITS WIDEST POINT. TWO MINOR INJURIES WERE SUFFERED AS HOME WAS DESTROYED NEAR SR 14. DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED FROM JUST EAST OF SELMA TO THE BURNSVILLE COMMUNITY.

*** 2 INJ *** AN EF2 TORNADO PRODUCED DAMAGE FOR 5.08 MILES AND WAS 1000 YARDS WIDE AT ITS WIDEST POINT. TWO PEOPLE SUFFERED MINOR INJURIES. THE TORNADO BEGAN JUST EAST OF SELMA AND LIFTED NEAR BURNSVILLE. MANY STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED AND NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN.
HEAVY RAINFALL IN A SHORT PERIOD OF TIME WAS PRODUCING SOME MINOR FLOODING IN HUEYTOWN.
20 TO 30 TREES BLOWN DOWN AND MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE NEAR HIGHWAY 17 AND WALKER ROAD.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 4 NNE WARD. 20 TO 30 TREES BLOWN DOWN AND MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE NEAR HIGHWAY 17 AND WALKER ROAD. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
SEVERAL TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 83, HIGHWAY 84, HIGHWAY 528, AND HIGHWAY 26. AT LEAST ONE STRUCTURE WAS DAMAGED. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

A NWS SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED DAMAGE FROM AN EF1 TORNADO THAT BEGAN ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF HIGHWAY 28E JUST EAST OF LIVINGSTON AND ENDED NEAR PORT OF EPES HIGHWAY ON APPROACH TO THE TOMBIGBEE RIVER. NUMEROUS HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. A MOBILE HOME WAS SHIFTED FROM ITS BLOCKS, WITH OTHER STRUCTURAL DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH CONSISTING OF SECTIONS OF MISSING ROOFING MATERIAL AND DESTROYED OUTBUILDINGS.
SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND AT LEAST 4 STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED IN BLUFFPORT. DAMAGE WAS REPORTED AT HIGHWAY 21 AND VALLEY ROAD. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

AN EF1 TORNADO PRODUCED DAMAGE FOR 2.87 MILES AND WAS 150 YARDS WIDE AT ITS WIDEST POINT. DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED FROM TOM MINER ROAD EAST OF BILLINGSLEY TO JUST NORTH OF CR 24 BEFORE CR 19. MULTIPLE TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND AT LEAST ONE STRUCTURE WAS DAMAGED NEAR HIGHWAY 24 AND HIGHWAY 34. ADDITIONAL DAMAGE WAS REPORTED ALONG HIGHWAY 53.
MULTIPLE TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND AT LEAST ONE STRUCTURE WAS DAMAGED NEAR HIGHWAY 24 AND HIGHWAY 34. ADDITIONAL DAMAGE WAS REPORTED ALONG HIGHWAY 53.

AN EF1 TORNADO PRODUCED DAMAGED FOR 3.04 MILES AND WAS 500 YARDS WIDE AT ITS WIDEST POINT. THE TORNADO WAS EAST OF CLANTON AND PRODUCED SOME STRUCTURAL DAMAGE AND KNOCKED NUMEROUS TREES DOWN. FURTHER EVALUATION IS ONGOING.
BASEBALL AND GOLFBALL SIZE HAIL IN GORDO. RELAYED THROUGH SOCIAL MEDIA PICTURES.
TORNADO REPORTED ON THE GROUND AND CAUSING DAMAGE EAST OF CLANTON ON HIGHWAY 491. RADAR ALSO HAD A TDS AT THIS LOCATION.

A NWS SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED A BRIEF EF0 TORNADO THAT CROSSED LOCK AND DAM ROAD JUST WEST OF FOSCUE PARK, OUTSIDE OF THE CITY OF DEMOPOLIS. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF UPROOTED TREES, LARGE TREE BRANCHES BROKEN, AND SOME PANELING PULLED FROM A BUILDING ADJACENT TO FOSCUE CREEK.
TORNADO OBSERVED VIA TV STATION WEBCAM BROADCAST WITH POWER FLASH AND STORM CHASER DRONE VIDEO.

AN EF1 TORNADO PRODUCED DAMAGE FOR 22.03 MILES AND WAS 550 YARDS WIDE AT ITS WIDEST POINT. THE TORNADO STARTED IN MARTIN SLOUGH EAST OF THE WARRIOR RIVER AND LIFTED NNW OF DUNCANVILLE. MODERATE STRUCTURE DAMAGE AND TREE DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG THE PATH. SEVERAL BUSINESSES DAMAGED IN MOUNDVILLE.
TORNADO LIKELY FROM TDS ON LOCAL RADAR NEAR WEOGUFKA.

NWS METEOROLOGISTS SURVEYED DAMAGE SOUTH OF SWEET WATER AND DETERMINED THAT IT WAS CONSISTENT WITH AN EF1 TORNADO. THE TORNADO BEGAN ALONG MARENGO COUNTY 6. IT CONTINUED NORTHEASTWARD AND REACHED ITS MAXIMUM INTENSITY ALONG MARENGO COUNTY 7. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED AS IT CROSSED HIGHWAY 10 JUST SOUTHEAST OF SWEET WATER. DAMAGE WAS LIMITED TO NUMEROUS SNAPPED TREES. THIS WAS THE FIRST OF TWO TORNADOES IN THE SWEET WATER AREA THAT AFTERNOON.
ROOF DAMAGE TO A NUMBER OF HOUSES IN THE CITY ON BOTH SIDES OF THE COUNTY LINE. SEVERAL BUSINESSES DAMAGED IN MOUNDVILLE.
TORNADO REPORTED BY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT NEAR BIRMINGPORT.

NWS METEOROLOGISTS SURVEYED DAMAGE AROUND COOSA COUNTY 56 AND DETERMINED THAT IT WAS CAUSED BY A BRIEF EF0 TORNADO. DAMAGE WAS LIMITED TO AN UPROOTED PINE TREE AND SEVERAL TREES HAD BROKEN BRANCHES. A BRIEF TDS WAS ALSO NOTED ON BMX RADAR.
MULTIPLE HOMES DAMAGED IN THE WATERBURY SUBDIVISION.
AN EF1 TORNADO PRODUCED DAMAGE FOR 0.53 MILES AND WAS 150 YARDS WIDE AT ITS WIDEST POINT. MINOR STRUCTURE AND TIMBER DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG THE PATH.
DAMAGE REPORTED IN THE WILDWOOD COMMUNITY. DETAILS UNKNOWN.

VILLAGE CREEK AT AVENUE W WENT INTO FLOOD STAGE AND CAUSED MINOR FLOODING IN ENSLEY. ADDITIONALLY, THERE WERE SEVERAL ROADS IN THE BIRMINGHAM METRO AREA THAT WERE TEMPORARILY IMPASSABLE DUE TO HIGH WATER.
THE TORNADO PRODUCED DAMAGE FOR 3.51 MILES AND WAS 650 YARDS WIDE AT ITS WIDEST POINT. THE TORNADO PRODUCED SIGNIFICANT TIMBER DAMAGE , SOME STRUCTURE DAMAGE AND DAMAGE TO A COMMUNICATIONS TOWER.
REPORT FROM MPING: 3-INCH TREE LIMBS BROKEN; POWER POLES BROKEN.
SEVERAL ROADS WERE FLOODED AND TEMPORARILY IMPASSABLE AROUND SWEETWATER.
REPORTED BY SPOTTER WHO WAS SE OF THIS LOCATION, LOOKING NW.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE METEOROLOGISTS SURVEYED DAMAGE JUST EAST OF CENTRAL MILLS AND FOUND IT CONSISTENT WITH A TORNADO. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR AL HIGHWAY 66 W WHERE SEVERAL LARGE BRANCHES WERE SNAPPED. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST THROUGH PASTURE LANDS WHERE ADDITIONAL MINOR TREE DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED. THE TORNADO THEN BEGAN TO LIFT JUST NORTH OF COUNTY ROAD 109 WHERE AN ADDITIONAL SOFTWOOD TREE WAS UPROOTED.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 1 W MAYTOWN. A NWS SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED EF-1 TORNADO DAMAGE IN THE MAYTOWN AREA OF JEFFERSON COUNTY. TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED, WITH RELATIVELY MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. THE DAMAGE PATH STARTED ALONG CROCKER ROAD AND ENDED SHORTLY AFTER THE TORNADO CROSSED TO THE EAST SIDE OF SHADY GROVE ROAD ON APPROACH TO BAYVIEW LAKE.

THIS LONG TRACK TORNADO BEGAN IN THE NWS MOBILE FORECAST AREA AND TRAVELED ALMOST 35 MILES THROUGH WAYNE, CHOCTAW, AND CLARKE COUNTIES AS AN EF2 TORNADO. IT WEAKENED TO AN EF1 TORNADO AS IT CROSSED INTO MARENGO COUNTY. IT CONTINUED TO SNAP AND UPROOT NUMEROUS TREES AS IT CROSSED HIGHWAY 69. THE TORNADO DISSIPATED IN A WOODED AREA NORTHEAST OF HIGHWAY 69.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN ALONG HWY 69.

NWS METEOROLOGISTS SURVEYED DAMAGE IN AND NORTH OF SWEET WATER AND DETERMINED THAT IT WAS CONSISTENT WITH AN EF0 TORNADO. THE TORNADO BEGAN NEAR 3RD AVENUE IN SWEET WATER AND CONTINUED NORTH CROSSING HIGHWAY 10. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTH OF SWEET WATER, DISSIPATING NEAR BEAVER CREEK ROAD. DAMAGE MAINLY CONSISTED OF BROKEN LARGE TREE BRANCHES. THIS WAS THE SECOND TORNADO NEAR SWEET WATER THAT AFTERNOON.
DAMAGE REPORTED ALONG NICHOLSVILLE RD. TREES DOWN AND 1 MOBILE HOME DAMAGED.

*** 1 INJ *** CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 1 SSE MOUNT OLIVE. A NWS SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED EF-0 TORNADO DAMAGE IN THE MOUNT OLIVE AREA OF JEFFERSON COUNTY. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF DOWNED TREES WITH RELATIVELY MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE CAUSED DIRECTLY BY WIND. HOWEVER, THERE WERE INSTANCES OF SIGNIFICANT RESIDENTIAL DAMAGE AND VEHICLE DAMAGE FROM FALLEN TREES. THE DAMAGE PATH STARTED NEAR SUTHERLAND ROAD ON THE WEST SIDE OF INTERSTATE 65 AND ENDED NEAR STOUTS ROAD ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE INTERSTATE.
POWER LINES/POLES DOWN IN THE 2400 BLOCK OF MT. OLIVE RD.
DAMAGE REPORTED ALONG HWY 31. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

THE TORNADO PRODUCED DAMAGE FOR 5.84 MILES AND WAS 400 YARDS WIDE AT ITS WIDEST POINT. THE TORNADO STARTED NEAR THE SWAMPY AREA ALONG LITTLE MULBERRY CREEK NORTHWEST OF BILLINGSLEY. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST EAST OF CR 16, EAST OF POOLS CROSSROADS. SEVERAL STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED AND A FEW WERE DESTROYED, NUMEROUS TREES WERE DOWNED.
HOMES DAMAGED IN THE POOLS CROSSROADS AREA.

A NWS SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED DAMAGE FROM AN EF-1 TORNADO THAT AFFECTED LOCATIONS JUST NORTHWEST OF ONEONTA IN BLOUNT COUNTY. THE TORNADO BEGAN NEAR TIM KING ROAD ALONG HUNT MOUNTAIN, MOVED NORTHEAST, AND ENDED NEAR KENT ROAD JUST EAST OF HWY 75. DAMAGE MAINLY CONSISTED OF SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES.
TREES DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 231 AND CR 33. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
STRUCTURAL DAMAGE ALONG PROMISE LANE. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
REPORT FROM MPING: STREET/ROAD FLOODING; STREET/ROAD CLOSED; VEHICLES STRANDED.
ROOF BLOWN OFF OF TRACTOR BARN.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE METEOROLOGISTS SURVEYED DAMAGE SOUTH OF WHITE CITY AND FOUND IT CONSISTENT WITH A TORNADO. THE TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN ALONG COUNTY ROAD 43 JUST WEST OF COUNTY ROAD 21 WHERE A METAL OUTBUILDING WAS DESTROYED, A COUPLE SOFTWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED, AND LARGE BRANCHES WERE BROKEN. THE TORNADO ALSO CAUSED DAMAGE TO A NEARBY CARPORT WHICH WAS LOFTED INTO AN OPEN FIELD. THE TORNADO BRIEFLY CONTINUED NORTHEAST INTO AN OPEN PASTURE WHERE IT LIFTED.
REPORT FROM MPING: RIVER/CREEK OVERFLOWING; CROPLAND/YARD/BASEMENT FLOODING.
A FEW TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG CR 20.
A FEW TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF GARDNER ROAD AND UPPER KINGSTON ROAD.
SHINGLES BLOWN OF ROOFS AND FENCES BLOWN DOWN IN BOYKIN LAKES NEIGHBORHOOD.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN BRIEFLY ON THE EAST SIDE OF HIGHWAY 231 ON THE NORTH SIDE OF PINE LEVEL IN MONTGOMERY COUNTY WEDNESDAY EVENING. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON A FARMSTEAD UPROOTING TREES IN THE FRONT YARD, DESTROYING A PLAYHOUSE AND TRAMPOLINE. A TREE FELL ON THE HOUSE DAMAGING THE ROOF. ADDITIONAL STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS ALSO NOTED TO THE ROOF MAINLY IN THE FORM OF SHINGLE LOSS. THE EF-0 TORNADO CONTINUED TO MOVE NORTHEAST ACROSS THE FARM PROPERTY UPROOTING MAINLY SOFTWOOD TREES WITH SOME SPORADIC SNAPPING OF TREES ALONG ITS PATH. A FENCE ON THE PROPERTY ALSO SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE. THE TORNADO LIFTED ABRUPTLY NEAR THE BACK PASTURELAND ABOUT A 1/3 OF A MILE INTO ITS LIFE CYCLE.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN OFF OF HIGHWAY 231.
LARGE PINE TREES DOWN ON POWERLINES. LIVE WIRES DOWN AND ROAD IMPASSABLE.
CALDWELL MILL AT PAHOKEE TRACE IS BLOCKED BY DOWNED TREES.
A BRIEF PERIOD OF DIME TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL. VERY FEW LARGER SIZE PIECES. OCCURRED JUST EAST OF BORDEN SPRINGS.
SOCIAL MEDIA VIDEO OF HAIL AT LEAST QUARTER SIZE NEAR GARDENDALE.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS HAIL REPORT FROM 1 SSE GARDENDALE. SOCIAL MEDIA PICTURE OF HAIL AT LEAST GOLF BALL SIZE NEAR GARDENDALE.
SOCIAL MEDIA PHOTOS OF QUARTER SIZE HAIL NEAR PINSON.
AN EF2 TORNADO DOWNED NUMEROUS TREES ACROSS NORTHEAST HALE COUNTY, EXTENDING JUST ACROSS THE BORDER INTO SOUTHEAST TUSCALOOSA COUNTY.
CONFIRMED TORNADO PER TDS OFF OF KBMX RADAR.

*** 5 INJ *** THE EF3 TORNADO ACROSS NORTHERN SHELBY COUNTY BEGAN IN NORTHERN BIBB COUNTY WITH DAMAGE UP TO EF2 IN MAGNITUDE. FIVE PEOPLE WERE INJURED, ALL IN NORTHERN SHELBY COUNTY. TOTAL PATH LENGTH WAS 50 MILES.
CONFIRMED TORNADO PER TDS OFF OF KBMX RADAR.
NUMEROUS TREES UPROOTED AND POWER LINES DOWNED ON OLD COAL CITY ROAD.
A STRONG TORNADO TRACKED 34 MILES ACROSS NORTHERN SHELBY COUNTY JUST INTO ST. CLAIR COUNTY, DAMAGING OR DESTROYING NUMEROUS HOMES. THE TORNADO REACHED EF3 INTENSITY IN THE EAGLE POINT NEIGHBORHOOD.
CONFIRMED TORNADO PER TDS OFF OF KBMX RADAR.
SEVERAL TREES UPROOTED AT THE PELHAM HIGH SCHOOL. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
NUMEROUS TREES UPROOTED NEAR HIGHWAY 280 AND DOUG BAKER BLVD. TREES BLOCKING HIGHWAY 280.
NUMEROUS STRUCTURES REPORTED DAMAGED IN THE EAGLE POINT AND GREYSTONE SUBDIVISIONS.
AN EF1 TORNADO CROSSED THE STATE LINE FROM MISSISSIPPI AND SNAPPED AND UPROOTED NUMEROUS TREES AS IT MOVED NORTHEASTWARD ACROSS HIGHWAY 32. A RESIDENCE ALSO SUFFERED PARTIAL ROOF DAMAGE.
*** 5 FATAL *** SUBSTANTIAL DAMAGE TO PROPERTY AND HOMES IN THE CITY OF OHATCHEE. CORONER CONFIRMS 5 FATALITIES. UPDATE FOR FATALITY COUNT.
SUBSTANTIAL DAMAGE TO PROPERTY AND HOMES IN THE CITY OF OHATCHEE. MEDIA REPORTS OF MULTIPLE FATALITIES.
SEVERAL HOMES DAMAGED ALONG OLD SURPHUR SPRINGS ROAD. NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES DOWNED.
*** 5 FATAL *** A STRONG EF2 TORNADO TRACKED FOR 24 MILES ACROSS CALHOUN COUNTY, DAMAGING OR DESTROYING NUMEROUS RESIDENCES.
CONFIRMED TORNADO PER TDS OFF OF KBMX RADAR.
HOMES DAMAGED IN WELLINGTON.
AN EF0 TORNADO CAUSED MAINLY TREE DAMAGE IN FAR SOUTHERN CHEROKEE COUNTY NEAR PIEDMONT.
STRUCTURAL DAMAGE AND TREES DOWN. REPORTED BY EMA.
AN EF1 TORNADO SNAPPED AND UPROOTED NUMEROUS TREES FROM COUNTY ROAD 174 NORTHWEST OF EUTAW TO COUNTY ROAD 218 SOUTHWEST OF KNOXVILLE.
FLASH FLOODING ALONG HWY 411 AND CR 101. ADDITIONAL FLASH FLOODING WAS OBSERVED ACROSS THE COUNTY BETWEEN 410 PM AND 630 PM. REPORTED BY EMERGENCY MANAGER.
AN EF3 TORNADO TRACKED FOR 80 MILES ACROSS HALE, PERRY, BIBB, CHILTON AND SHELBY COUNTIES. COUNTLESS TREES WERE DOWNED AND HUNDREDS OF STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED.

AN EF1 TORNADO FORMED IN THE SOUTH EASTLAKE AND SOUTH HIGHLANDS NEIGHBORHOODS OF NORTHEAST BIRMINGHAM. IT CONTINUED NORTHEAST INTO THE SOUTH ROEBUCK AND ROEBUCK PLAZA NEIGHBORHOODS, SNAPPING AND UPROOTING NUMEROUS TREES BEFORE DISSIPATING IN SOUTHWEST TRUSSVILLE. AROUND 42 HOMES WERE DAMAGED, ALMOST ALL BY FALLING TREES.
10-20 HOMES HEAVILY DAMAGED NEAR WILLOW LANE AND ROEBUCK FOREST DRIVE.
TRESS UPROOTED IN THE CITY OF ROEBUCK. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
ROAD IMPASSABLE BEHIND MAYTOWN FIRE STATION.
HOMES SIGNIFICANTLY DAMAGED ALONG BELCHER ROAD IN BRENT.
TREES DOWN ALONG THE INTERSTATE NEAR MM229. REPORTED BY OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE.
REPORT OF HOMES DAMAGED IN COLUMBIANA. RELAYED BY MEDIA.
SEVERAL TRAILERS DAMAGED AND TREES DOWN FROM RIVERVIEW LANE NEAR HIGHWAY 10 EASTWARD ACROSS HIGHWAY 69. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
A TORNADO MOVED INTO MARENGO COUNTY FROM CHOCTAW COUNTY AND STRENGTHENED INTO AN EF2 TORNADO, DOWNING NUMEROUS TREES AND DAMAGING OR DESTROYING SEVERAL STRUCTURES.
WARRIOR JASPER ROAD IMPASSABLE.
LASTED ABOUT 3 MINUTES.
AN EF2 TORNADO MOVES THROUGH EASTERN CHILTON AND WESTERN COOSA COUNTIES, DAMAGING HOMES ALONG LAKE MITCHELL AND DOWNING NUMEROUS TREES.
DIME TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL REPORTED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
TDS DETECTED ON RADAR. MULTIPLE STRUCTURES DAMAGED ON CR 481 ON CARGILE CREEK. ADDITIONAL STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED ALONG CR 483 AND ON CR 720. NUMEROUS TREES WERE ALSO DOWNED.
AN EF2 TORNADO MOVED ACROSS PORTIONS OF CLAY AND RANDOLPH COUNTIES FOR ALMOST 28 MILES, DAMAGING RESIDENCES AND DOWNING NUMEROUS TREES.
MULTIPLE REPORTS OF DAMAGE TO HOMES IN FAR SOUTHEAST CLAY COUNTY. LOCATION APPROXIMATE.
TREES DOWN AND DAMAGE TO HOMES NEAR CORINTH.
DIME TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL IN BOLDO. ALSO SEVERAL TREES DOWN.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL AND TREE DOWN IN PINEYWOODS.

REPORT OF A SWATH OF TREES DOWN WITH SOME TREES SNAPPED, MOSTLY FALLING IN THE SAME DIRECTION. PEA SIZE HAIL WAS ALSO REPORTED. EVENT TIME REPORTED WAS 4:53 PM WHILE BY RADAR THE TIME WAS CLOSER TO 4:59 PM. DELAYED REPORT RECEIVED THROUGH SOCIAL MEDIA.
TREES DOWN ON JOE NAIL ROAD.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL SOUTH OF MORRIS.
HAIL UP TO THE SIZE OF QUARTERS IN THE TOWN OF KENNEDY.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 2 NW TUCKER. MULTIPLE TREES DOWN BETWEEN BRILLIANT AND WINFIELD.
TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ON HIGHWAY 119 NORTH OF GRANTS MILL ROAD AND ALONG REX LAKE ROAD.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN BETWEEN BRILLIANT AND WINFIELD.

AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN SOUTH OF HAYDEN WITH THE MAJORITY OF THE DAMAGE CONSISTING OF DOWNED TREES. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 1.63 MILES. THE MAX WIDTH WAS 90 YARDS AND MAX WINDS WERE 80S MPH.

AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE NESMITH COMMUNITY. A FEW STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED AND MULTIPLE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 1.02 MILES IN WINSTON CO BEFORE DISSIPATING JUST INSIDE CULLMAN COUNTY. THE MAX WIDTH WAS 305 YARDS AND MAX WINDS WERE 90 MPH.
STRUCTURAL DAMAGE PHOTOS POSTED ON SOCIAL MEDIA FROM BROADCAST MEDIA. POSSIBLE TORNADO. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
AT LEAST SIX INCHES OF FLOWING WATER REPORTED IN GUIN IN SOME PLACES. REPORT VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
REPORTS OF A WATER RESCUE DUE TO FLASH FLOODING IN NAUVOO. CREEKS ARE OUT OF THEIR BANKS.

GUIN PD REPORTS A SECTION OF 15TH. AVE HAS WASHED AWAY NEAR THE SOFTBALL FIELD. CREEKS PASSING UNDER HWY. 43 ARE CONTINUING TO RISE & COULD POSSIBLY BEGIN TO SPILL OUT INTO THE ROAD SOON. RELAYED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.

A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF ARMSTRONG LOOP AND RUSSELL DR WITH DAMAGE CONSISTING MAINLY OF DOWNED TREES. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 0.21 MILES. THE MAX WIDTH WAS 65 YARDS AND MAX WINDS WERE 80 MPH.
SOCIAL MEDIA PHOTOS AND VIDEO ON FACEBOOK AND TWITTER VIA BROADCAST MEDIA SHOWS TREES DOWN. POSSIBLE TORNADO. TIME AND LOCATION VERY APPROXIMATE ACCORDING TO RADAR.

FLOODING ALONG VALLEY TRAIL, ARMSTRONG LOOP ROAD. ONE TREE REPORTED DOWN IN THIS AREA THAT FELL ONTO A SHED. ALSO IN ONEONTA, FLOODING 1ST AVENUE EAST TO 132 AND AT HIGH ROCK ROAD NEAR HAYDEN. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
SECTION OF WINDHAM LOOP WASHED OUT BY RUSHING WATER.

AN EF-0 TORNADO TRACK WAS CONFIRMED BY NWS BMX SURVEY TEAM FROM NORTHEAST PERRY COUNTY TO SOUTHWEST CHILTON COUNTY. THE TORNADO BEGAN ALONG J MITCHELL ROAD AND MOVED EAST-NORTHEASTWARD BEFORE TAKING A RIGHT-HAND JOG AND ENDING JUST SOUTH OF MAPLESVILLE. DAMAGE CONSISTED PRIMARILY OF SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES.

WIND DAMAGE WAS PREVALENT ALONG A LINE OF STORMS THAT MOVED THROUGH AUTAUGA AND ELMORE COUNTIES, MAKING IT HARD TO DISTINGUISH BETWEEN STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE AND TORNADIC DAMAGE; HOWEVER, A CORRIDOR OF ENHANCED DAMAGE, CO-LOCATED WITH A ROTATIONAL RADAR SIGNATURE, WAS APPARENT ALONG A PATH FROM PRATTVILLE TO MILLBROOK. THIS WAS ASSOCIATED WITH AN EF-1 TORNADO. STRUCTURAL DAMAGE CAUSED DIRECTLY BY WIND CONSISTED OF SOME SHINGLE LOSS, INCLUDING: MANY RESIDENCES, THE BASS PRO SHOP ON THE WEST SIDE OF I-65, AND SEVERAL HOTELS ON THE EAST SIDE OF I-65 AT EXIT 179. A CHEVRON ALSO HAD MANY METAL PANELS BLOWN FROM THE GAS PUMP CANOPY. SOME HOMES WERE VERY HEAVILY DAMAGED BY LARGE, FALLEN TREES. THERE WERE A FEW LOCATIONS WHERE THE NUMBER / DENSITY OF SNAPPED HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES INCREASED, WHICH PUSHED THE RATING TO EF-1. UPROOTED TREES WERE PLENTIFUL AS WELL.

NWS STORM SURVEY PERSONNEL FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-0 TORNADO IN THE ANTIOCH COMMUNITY SOUTHEAST OF TROY, AL. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR WHITE WATER CREEK RD AND TRAVELED NORTHEAST WHILE KNOCKING DOWN A FEW TREES AND TREE LIMBS. THE TORNADO THEN MOVED MORE NORTHWARD AND CROSSED US HWY 231 WHERE A FEW FOLKS OBTAINED VIDEO FOOTAGE OF THE TORNADO. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR PARRON CHURCH RD.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 1 WNW ANTIOCH. FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED A TORNADO AT CR 3304 AND HIGHWAY 231. NO DAMAGE REPORTED AT THIS TIME. SEVERAL VIDEOS WERE RECEIVED OF THE TORNADO.
FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED A TORNADO AT CR 3304 AND HIGHWAY 231. NO DAMAGE REPORTED AT THIS TIME.

NWS STORM SURVEY PERSONNEL FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO NEAR RED RD, GENERALLY EAST SOUTHEAST OF TUSKEGEE, AL. A FEW TREES AND TREE LIMBS WERE BLOWN DOWN, AND ONE RESIDENCE SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE TO SIDING AND ROOFING MATERIAL.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 3 NNW CLAUD. A LOW TOPPED SUPERCELL PRODUCED A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO ALONG PLEASANT HILL ROAD ABOUT 8.9 MILES NORTHEAST OF WETUMPKA. ABOUT A DOZEN LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED SPORADICALLY ALONG THE PATH IN A CONVERGENT PATTERN NEAR THE CENTER OF THE PATH, WITH WEAKER TREE LIMB DAMAGE ON THE OUTSKIRTS OF THE PATH. A TREE FELL ON A BARN. ONE MANUFACTURED HOME HAD A ROOF PARTIALLY REMOVED AS WELL AS MINOR DAMAGE TO A DECK AND SKIRTING.

A VERY BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO OCCURRED ON THE NORTHERN SIDE OF ALBRITTON LANE JUST NORTHEAST OF MONTGOMERY. ONE HOME HAD A SECTION OF ROOFING AND SOFFIT BLOWN OFF AND ANOTHER HOME HAD MANY SHINGLES REMOVED. TWO SEPARATE SECTIONS OF FENCING WERE KNOCKED OVER AS WELL. ANOTHER HOME HAD SOME ROOFING BLOWN OFF A BACK PORCH. OBSERVED DAMAGE, DEBRIS TRAJECTORY RELATIVE TO THE STORMS PATH, AND SMALL PIECES OF DEBRIS IN TREES INDICATED A CIRCULATION AND LOFTING.
